beats.narration: Every penetrant test answers to a document. Before the first drop touches the part, somebody chose the standard governing the job.

payload.title: PT STANDARDS & CODES
payload.sub: Know which rulebook you're inside before you spray.

beats.narration: Start with the umbrella. ASTM E165 covers the whole method: both penetrant types, all four removal methods. ASME Section Five, Article Six adopts it as SE-165.
Beneath it, process-specific practices each nail one fluorescent line. Cite E1210 and you know, without reading further, you're running hydrophilic remover, Method D.
Requirements flow down a chain. Contract names the code and revision, the code sets the method, your procedure becomes the steps you work to.
And the line people miss: the practice tells you how to test. The contract tells you whether it passes. Never pull accept-reject limits from a practice.

payload.sub: Acceptance criteria live with the referencing code, never the how-to.
